data.content:
**Yes, C# fully supports `stackalloc`'d `Span` of native-sized integers.** Both `Span<nint>` and `Span<nuint>` work:

```csharp
Span<nint>  signed   = stackalloc nint[4];   // ✅
Span<nuint> unsigned = stackalloc nuint[4];  // ✅
```

Tested with .NET 10 — compiles and runs cleanly. On this 64-bit system, `nint.Size` reports **8 bytes**. This has been supported since C# 9 / .NET 5, when `nint`/`nuint` were introduced as first-class types (aliases for `System.IntPtr`/`System.UIntPtr`).
